behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at watcher->OS event boundary (recursive watch behavior), not just local parsing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can simulate subdirectory paths but can miss real non-recursive watcher configuration and OS event propagation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is a single deterministic filesystem-watch outcome, not AI output or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory and write a file inside it, then assert the transformation output appears in the output directory without restart/manual trigger.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is core dedup decision logic (same path + time window), which can be exercised inside one component with injected clock/state. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess boundary in the behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because the behavior text is specifically 'deduplication logic' and does not require real watcher events or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test dedup component with fake clock: first request for path is accepted, second request within window is dropped, and request after window expires is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references OS duplicate filesystem events. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher callbacks can hide real event burst patterns/coalescing differences. Must validate real watcher+OS interaction end-to-end. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because this is not AI-dependent or a multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: With real filesystem watching enabled, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+flush/atomic-save) and assert only one processing/output record is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is watcher lifecycle under real directory delete/recreate OS events. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes often miss backend watcher invalidation/re-subscription behavior after inode/path replacement. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior is a single deterministic daemon resiliency path, not AI judgment or multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watch path, delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering uses filesystem creation timestamps under concurrent arrival, which depends on real FS metadata granularity/timing plus queueing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps can pass while real FS resolution/tie behavior causes misordering. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text defines deterministic timestamp ordering, not model-dependent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the watched directory on a real filesystem, record their creation timestamps, and assert daemon processing order matches timestamp order (including deterministic tie-break rule if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in real-time interaction between writer, OS events, and daemon readiness checks. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fakes usually cannot reproduce partial-write timing, file lock semantics, or flush visibility precisely. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because this is deterministic IO coordination, not AI or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real writer process/stream to write a file in chunks with delays while daemon watches directory; assert daemon does not process mid-write and only processes after final close/complete-write condition.
